$description: Named component grammar for the cinematic-scroll skill. Each component is token-driven (design.md), ships in both modes, and carries an inline degrade contract. Doctor-verified (Mode A scores >=80).

components.name: PinnedReveal
components.purpose: Staggered eyebrow → title → summary reveal as a chapter enters view — the long-take title arrival, on the --ease-reveal curve.
guardrails: §1.6 transform/opacity only
§4.1 custom easing (no ease/linear)
§1.9 reduced-motion safe
a11y: :focus-visible + <main> landmark
mobile breakpoint

components.name: HeroParallax
components.purpose: A hero "tracking shot" whose graded depth planes (far/back/mid/near) translate at different scroll rates so the frame reads as deep space, with a split-line title reveal — rAF-throttled parallax (or CSS scroll-timeline) that collapses to <=2 layers on mobile and goes fully static under reduced motion.
guardrails: §1.4 — no parallax on body copy; only background/decorative planes drift, the copy sits static at depth 1.0
§1.5 — never setState in a scroll handler; Mode B scrubs via ScrollTrigger and Mode A writes transform directly, never through React state
§1.6 — animate transform + opacity only; planes use translate3d/yPercent, title uses translateY + opacity, no width/height/top/left/filter
§1.7 — never more than 7 depth layers; max 4 desktop, collapsed to <=2 on mobile
§1.8 — never attach an unthrottled scroll listener; Mode A is rAF-throttled (one read + one write per frame) with a CSS scroll-timeline fast path, Mode B uses ScrollTrigger scrub
§1.9 / reduced-motion — prefers-reduced-motion:reduce disables all parallax and reveal and shows the static composition; no 3D tilt is used so touch is inherently safe
§4.3 — depth multipliers never repeat: 0.16 / 0.34 / 0.62 / 1.18 are graded and non-rhythmic
§4.7 — depth layers earn their place; the mobile collapse drops the atmosphere + foreground-accent planes that add no value at small sizes
§4.8 — typography breathes: --lh-tight 1.1 for display, max two families (display + ui)

components.name: DepthFigure
components.purpose: A foreground image card held in depth — rAF-throttled scroll parallax plus a gentle pointer tilt (small rotateX/rotateY) gated to hover + fine-pointer and disabled on touch / reduced-motion, with an alt-bearing img and a soft token shadow that degrade to a still, composed card.
guardrails: §1.9 — No 3D rotation (rotateX/rotateY/perspective tilt) on touch devices or under prefers-reduced-motion: tilt is gated behind @media (hover:hover) and (pointer:fine) in CSS and a matching matchMedia()/gsap.matchMedia() check in JS, so touch never gets 3D rotation; mobile keeps the touch-safe scroll parallax + entrance reveal alive (not a dead page).

§1.11 / §4 (taste / anti-convergence) — two distinct named eases (--ease-reveal decel for entrance + tilt-track, --ease-playful overshoot for tilt settle); no 'transition: all'; no literal ease/linear/ease-in-out.

Performance budget — transform + opacity only in every hot path (entrance, parallax, tilt); scroll work is rAF-throttled to one read + one write per frame (Mode A) / ScrollTrigger scrub (Mode B); will-change:transform only inside the fine-pointer branch; no width/height/top/left/filter/blur animation.

A11y —
landmark, aria-labelledby on the section, :focus-visible outline, carries descriptive alt + width/height for layout stability.
Reduced-motion — @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) (Mode A) / gsap.matchMedia no-preference branch (Mode B) disables entrance, parallax and tilt, leaving a still composed card.

components.name: TiltCard
components.purpose: A card that tilts up to ~8 degrees in 3D toward the pointer with an opacity-only specular sheen — gated to hover + fine-pointer, disabled on touch and reduced-motion, and easing back flat on leave.
guardrails: 1.6 transform/opacity only in the hot path — JS writes only CSS custom props consumed by transform: perspective()/rotateX/rotateY and the sheen's opacity; no width/height/top/left/filter, no transition:all
1.8 pointermove is rAF-throttled (one DOM write per frame in Mode A; GSAP quickTo internal rAF in Mode B) — never updates from a raw listener
1.9 3D rotation gated behind @media (hover:hover) and (pointer:fine), disabled on touch (pointer:coarse / ontouchstart) and under prefers-reduced-motion, with a stable flat-card fallback
1.11 role-varied easing — live tracking has no transition, the leave-settle uses --ease-playful / back.out(1.4) overshoot, the sheen fades on --ease-reveal
4.1 intentional custom cubic-bezier curves only (--ease-playful/--ease-reveal); no ease/linear/ease-in-out in any motion path
a11y floor — <main> landmark, aria-labelledby on the section, :focus-visible outline, prefers-reduced-motion branch in both CSS and JS

components.name: MorphBackground
components.purpose: A scroll chapter whose background morphs between a warm and a cool palette by crossfading two stacked full-bleed gradient layers via opacity — never animating background-color — while a fixed foreground palette keeps content AA-readable from top to floor.
guardrails: §1.1 no filter/blur animation — crossfade is opacity-only, no filter touched
§1.5 no setState in a scroll handler — Mode B writes one style.setProperty, never React state
§1.6 never animate width/height/top/left/margin/padding/background-color — only opacity animates; two gradient layers crossfade
§1.8 scroll listener is rAF-throttled (ticking guard), passive, with a CSS scroll-timeline native path on top
§1.9 no 3D rotation on touch — component has no tilt/pointer-3D at all, so nothing to gate
§1.11 / §4.1 no default easing — all motion uses var(--ease-reveal) / power3.out, never ease/linear/ease-in-out
§4.5 vary title treatment — eyebrow + summary rise (translate) while the title uses a clip/mask reveal
§4.6 palette temperature shifts across the chapter (warm before → cool after), not one temperature throughout
Reduced motion (§1.9 distinct state) — @media reduce + matchMedia freeze layer-b at opacity 0 and disable entrance motion, leaving a stable composed frame
AA contrast held — foreground palette is fixed and a constant scrim keeps type off the brightest gradient peaks

components.name: HorizontalGallery
components.purpose: A sticky section that converts vertical scroll progress into horizontal translateX of a keyboard-focusable card track on desktop, degrading to native horizontal overflow scroll (no pinning) on touch and under reduced-motion.
guardrails: 1.1 — no filter/blur animation anywhere; the only animated properties are transform and opacity
1.2 — no scroll-jacking of short content: on touch the pin is dropped and the track becomes a native overflow scroller
1.3 — single pinned section with a clear release viewport (spacers before/after), never a stacked pin chain
1.5 — no setState inside the scroll path; Mode A uses a rAF tick + raw style.transform, Mode B uses GSAP ScrollTrigger scrub (no React state)
1.6 — never animates width/height/top/left/margin/padding; horizontal travel is translate3d only
1.8 — scroll handler is rAF-throttled (single in-flight frame) and passive; preferred path is CSS animation-timeline with zero per-frame JS
1.9 — no 3D rotation / tilt at all, so nothing to gate; touch and reduced-motion get a static non-motion native scroller
1.10 — motion is purely scroll/user-driven, never autoplaying
5.3 — prefers-reduced-motion branch disables the pin and the scrub entirely, leaving a usable horizontal scroller

components.name: ScrubVideo
components.purpose: A scroll-scrubbed <video> whose currentTime is driven by scroll progress (rAF, requestVideoFrameCallback when available) inside a pinned stage, degrading on iOS/touch to a poster still (with an image-sequence canvas as the documented touch upgrade).
guardrails: §1.1 — no filter/blur/brightness animation in the scroll hot path (only opacity cross-fade + currentTime seek)
§1.5 — no setState/React state mutation inside the scroll handler; raw currentTime write via ref
§1.6 — no width/height/top/left/margin/padding animation; transform + opacity + currentTime only, no 'transition: all'
§1.8 — scroll listener is rAF/requestVideoFrameCallback-throttled with a ticking guard, passive: true
§1.9 — no 3D rotation; touch + prefers-reduced-motion both disable the scrub and rest on a stable poster
§1.10 — video never autoplays; motion is user-scroll-driven only (muted/playsinline/no autoplay attr)
§1.11 / §4.1 — two distinct curves used (--ease-reveal for entrance, --ease-exit for the poster cross-fade), no default ease/linear
§5.2-analog — first-class non-scrub fallback (poster still; documented image-sequence canvas upgrade) for iOS/touch where the effect is unsupported

components.name: KineticHeadline
components.purpose: A heading that splits into per-word, per-line spans which stagger in on transform + opacity as it enters view (split-line-rise vocabulary), reading as one accessible aria-label while the visual spans are aria-hidden, and collapsing to the plain heading under reduced motion.
guardrails: §1.6 — animates transform (translateY/yPercent) + opacity only; never width/height/top/left/margin/padding/filter, no transition:all
§1.9 — no 3D rotation/tilt anywhere; nothing to disable on touch, and prefers-reduced-motion collapses to the static plain heading (the only all-static state)
§3.5 — title reveal runs on --dur-slow (720ms), within the title-reveal duration band
§3.6 — per-word stagger of 70ms (--i * 70ms / stagger:0.07) kept to a ~5-step wave so early words don't finish before late ones start
§4.1 / §1.11 — uses the role-specific --ease-reveal cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1) (GSAP expo.out), never ease/linear/ease-in-out
§4.5 — implements the split-line-rise / word-stagger title treatment (each line's words rise from translateY behind a line-mask)
§4.8 — display type at line-height 1.1 (--lh-tight); a single display family, no third typeface
§1.5 — Mode B never calls setState in a scroll path; reveal is fired once by ScrollTrigger; Mode A flips a class via IntersectionObserver (no scroll handler)

components.name: MagneticCursor
components.purpose: A custom cursor dot that rAF-lerps toward the pointer and snaps toward real focusable magnetic targets on hover, gated to fine pointers and disabled (native cursor restored) on touch and reduced-motion.
guardrails: 1.5 — no setState inside the move/animation loop; the dot is mutated via direct transform / GSAP quickTo, never React state
1.6 — animates transform + opacity (and color) only; never width/height/top/left/margin/padding/filter
1.8 — a single rAF/gsap.ticker drives the lerp; pointermove only records coords (passive), no per-event layout work or unthrottled listener
1.9 — magnetic pointer effect is gated behind (hover:hover) and (pointer:fine), disabled under prefers-reduced-motion and on touch, with the native cursor as the stable non-motion fallback
1.11 — distinct easing roles: --ease-reveal for the dot's color/opacity fade vs --ease-playful / back.out for the snap scale (no single curve everywhere)
